headline: Morgan and Cohan Kick Off Monaco Fest with 'Dead City' Season 3 Premiere
lede: The zombie drama's international showcase signals a high-stakes return for the 'Walking Dead' spin-off franchise. Monte-Carlo Television Festival selects the series for its prestigious opening slot.
body:
AMC's post-apocalyptic drama 'The Walking Dead: Dead City' scores a premium international platform with its Season 3 world premiere at the Monte-Carlo Television Festival. The 65th edition of the prestigious event will screen the first two episodes, marking a significant moment for the spin-off series starring Jeffrey Dean Morgan and Lauren Cohan.

Showrunner Seth Hoffman's gritty urban zombie narrative continues to evolve, bringing Morgan's Negan and Cohan's Maggie into sharper narrative focus. The festival selection suggests continued global interest in the 'Walking Dead' universe, even as the original series concluded its landmark run.

The June 12-16 festival represents a strategic international rollout for the series, positioning 'Dead City' as a marquee television event. Morgan and Cohan's return provides a familiar anchor for fans while potentially attracting new audiences intrigued by the show's post-apocalyptic Manhattan setting.

With this Monte-Carlo premiere, the series signals its ongoing narrative ambition and international appeal. The festival's selection underscores the show's continued relevance in the competitive peak TV landscape.
key_facts: First two episodes of Season 3 to screen
Jeffrey Dean Morgan and Lauren Cohan attending
